steps.title: Lock in the goal
steps.content: The student wants to introduce Pauline Hopkins to an audience unfamiliar with her career. A good introduction needs (1) who she was, (2) the breadth of her career, and (3) at least one concrete notable work.

steps.title: Check which choice covers all three
steps.content: An audience unfamiliar with her needs identity (African American writer), scope (many genre-defining stories), and a representative achievement (the first African American mystery novel, Hagar's Daughter).

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Pauline Hopkins is the author of Hagar's Daughter...': gives only one novel and publication dates — never identifies her as an African American writer or signals her broader career. Overly narrow for an intro. ✗
  • Choice B — 'African American writer Pauline Hopkins created many genre-defining stories...': identifies her, names the scope of her career, and cites her landmark novel as an example. Complete intro. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Published in The Colored American Magazine from 1901 to 1902...': foregrounds publication logistics of one novel — doesn't introduce Hopkins or her career. Misses the goal. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Hagar's Daughter by Pauline Hopkins is considered the first African American mystery novel': gives the novel's significance but never says who Hopkins was or that she had a broader career. Too narrow. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
